scope_alignment.user_verbatim: Can the feedbackcoach be on top then normal company below. Such as: FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team
ba_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Title row and NC row computed display = 'flex'; NC row bounding-box top > title row bottom (stacked)
AC3: Both rows horizontally centered within header (each row's center within +/-2px of header's center)
AC4: No regression of Cycle 1 deliverables (H1='FeedbackCoach' 36px, NC circle 32x32 bg-blue-600, Briefcase tenure cards)
scope_alignment.scope_check: AC1 + AC2 cover the user's 'top/below' stacking ask on both viewports. AC3 explicitly covers Cycle 1 Section 5's 'Center the entire header section' mandate -- by asserting each row's horizontal center is within +/-2px of the header's center. AC4 prevents regression of Cycle 1 PASS criteria. Scope is neither narrowed nor expanded.
scope_alignment.centering_rationale: Each row keeps 'justify-center' which centers its children (Sparkles+h1 on row 1, NC+span on row 2) within the row. As block-level flex containers, both rows fill the header's content-width. Header retains 'text-center' (harmless on flex but kept for minimum-diff). Net result: both rows visually centered within the header, matching Cycle 1 Section 5 requirement.

investigation_completeness.concern_raised_by_orchestrator: flex defaults to flex-direction: row; a single inline-flex -> flex swap on a CONTAINER of multiple children does not change row direction; it just changes inline-vs-block layout of the container itself in its parent.
investigation_completeness.ba_response_correctly_identifies: The user's 'stack' requirement is about the TWO CONTAINERS (title-row div, NC-row div) stacking on top of each other, NOT about the children inside each container. The children inside each container (Sparkles+h1, NC+span) ARE supposed to stay on one row -- that's why flex-direction: row default is correct.

css_correctness_analysis.current_state: Both wrappers are 'inline-flex' -> they are inline-level boxes. The parent <header> is a normal block with 'text-center'. Two inline-level boxes in a block context flow inline (side-by-side) until they wrap. On desktop 1440px, total inline width fits, so they collapse onto one line.
css_correctness_analysis.after_fix_state: Both wrappers become 'flex' -> they are block-level boxes (the spec defines 'flex' display value as block-level flex container). Two block-level boxes in a block context stack vertically by default (one per line). Each box's CHILDREN are still laid out via flex-direction: row (default), so Sparkles+h1 stay on one row, NC+span stay on one row. The parent text-center has no effect on block-level boxes -- but justify-center on each flex container centers its own children.
css_correctness_analysis.stacking_mechanism: Block-level layout (the boxes naturally take their own line) -- NOT flex-direction: column. flex-col is unnecessary because the stacking is between SIBLING block-level boxes, not between FLEX ITEMS inside one container.
css_correctness_analysis.rationale_for_no_flex_col: flex-col would only matter if the user wanted the CHILDREN of one row to stack (e.g., Sparkles above h1). The user's mockup 'FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team' shows each line as a horizontal group; the slash separates the two GROUPS into two LINES. So row-direction inside each container is correct; only the two containers need to be block-level, which inline-flex -> flex achieves.

investigation_completeness.verification_method_proposed_by_ba: Playwright getComputedStyle(div).display === 'flex' for both rows + bounding-box assertion that NC row's top > title row's bottom. This is the correct test: it verifies both the property change AND the visual stacking outcome.

affected_file_accuracy.verification: Read app/page.tsx returned 45 lines. L7-28 contain DashboardHeader. L10 and L14 contain the exact inline-flex divs BA proposes to change. The two divs are siblings inside <header>, both wrapping the user-visible content the user pointed at ('FeedbackCoach' on row 1, 'NormalCompany - Marketing Team' on row 2). The proposed change targets exactly the right elements.

user_verbatim_complaint: Can the feedbackcoach be on top then normal company below. Such as: FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team

complaint_element_pointer.row2_classes_current: mt-2 inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500
complaint_element_pointer.row2_classes_proposed: mt-2 flex items-center justify-center gap-2 text-sm text-slate-500

summary: BA spec is correct: (a) line numbers L10/L14 verified by Read against actual app/page.tsx; (b) inline-flex -> flex on each row makes them block-level so they naturally stack vertically (two block boxes in a block parent), no flex-col needed because stacking is between SIBLING containers not children; (c) justify-center on each container preserves horizontal centering of each row's contents; (d) AC1-AC4 cover the user's stacking ask + Cycle 1 Section 5 centering preservation + Cycle 1 deliverable non-regression. The two-substitution diff is the minimum-correct fix.
